purpose: Engine-neutral canonical system map for Mind Warp Forge Phase 0.

systems.purpose: Immutable evidence, content-addressed objects, authority separation, replay, and recovery.
systems.proof: Deterministic replay, forged-authority rejection, backup/recovery, and corruption tests.

systems.purpose: Capture conversation evidence, expose source health, classify material knowledge for retrieval, and provide compact task context without treating chat as authority.
systems.proof: Versioned long-corpus replay, source-gap detection, legacy migration, portable path rejection, SQLite handle release, deterministic multi-facet knowledge routing, typed actor/category search, noise suppression, duplicate, correction, and approval-adversarial tests.

systems.purpose: Create bounded research briefs, source records, claims, contradictions, and discriminating experiments.
systems.proof: Citation traceability, cache/retry behavior, contradictory-source handling, and source-gap receipts.

systems.purpose: Move work packages through research, design, adversarial review, readiness, verification, promotion, and rollback.
systems.proof: Dependency, authority-lane, lifecycle, owner-brief, and failed-gate routing tests.

systems.purpose: Make canonical world, asset, scheduler, candidate, test, and failure state visible through inspectors and test viewports.
systems.proof: Every registry system has an evidence-linked inspector and reproducible test visualization.

systems.purpose: One seeded universe, hierarchical addresses, generator versions, deterministic random streams, and stable object identities.
systems.proof: Strict canonical encoding, byte-exact fixed vectors, stream partitioning, migration, malformed-input, collision, boundary, and authority-negative tests; cross-platform receipts remain outstanding.

systems.purpose: Reusable mathematical basis functions, transforms, field packets, and cacheable recipes.
systems.proof: Philox known-answer, strict recipe bytes, repeatability, partitioning, cell continuity, composition, poison/overflow, cache binding, authority-negative, and read-only ProofReceipt integration tests; second-platform and expanded quality/cost receipts remain open.

systems.purpose: Derive stellar, geological, climate, terrain, resource, and environmental conditions from field packets.
systems.proof: Causal consistency, output-range, cross-seed, and regional-variation tests.

systems.purpose: Addressable, lazy materialisation from galaxy to entity with cache and promotion policy.
systems.proof: Strict deterministic descriptors, explicit origin, stable finite windows capped at 256, stale/cancel/budget rejection, and residency-negative identity fixtures; production generation and cross-platform receipts remain open.

systems.purpose: Baseline reconstruction plus explicit mutable player and world deltas.
systems.proof: Canonical baseline binding, per-target ordered/idempotent replay, conflict/gap/corruption rejection, snapshot distrust, old/new generator coexistence, explicit migration, and authority-negative read-only ProofReceipt fixtures; production storage and cross-target transactions remain open.

systems.purpose: Shared importance packet for rendering, simulation, AI, physics, animation, audio, streaming, and generation.
systems.proof: Strict packet encoding, asymmetric hysteresis, protection, monotone independent consumer maps, and authority-negative fixtures; product signals, weights, and mappings remain open.

systems.purpose: Predictive prefetch, pinning, cancellation, deadlines, fallback, and CPU/GPU/I/O/main-thread budgets.
systems.proof: Strict tickets, graph validation, safety admission, live priority donation, bounded fairness, deterministic traces, cancellation settlement, stale-output quarantine, fallback, and read-only ProofReceipt fixtures; runtime controllers, executors, cache policy, and performance remain open.

systems.purpose: Derive roles, competing solution families, concepts, and capabilities from world pressures and gameplay needs.
systems.proof: Strict canonical records, label-independent concept identity, grounded acyclic justification, mechanism-evidence diversity, hard feasibility, bounded validation, poison text, homonym, cycle, drift, and authority-negative fixtures; product ontology and generation remain open.

systems.purpose: Universal part-role graphs, typed sockets, geometry operations, articulation, deformation, material regions, and validators.
systems.proof: Closed capability registry, typed part/socket graphs, connectivity and compatibility failures, exact ordered atomic recipe replay, stale/dangling rejection, deterministic fingerprints, and authority-negative fixtures; geometry, representation, physical validation, and runtime remain open.

systems.purpose: Choose a neutral representation family from grounded functional requirements and explicit alternatives without category, format, renderer, or engine defaults.
systems.proof: Strict portfolios, content-bound lineage, hostile-reference rejection, P5-bound temporal plans, P7b-0 controlled-review protocol validation, and a deterministic built-in data-only wireframe viewport pass with read-only integration; general geometry, materials, rendering, perception, assets, animation, and external runtime compatibility remain open.

systems.purpose: Produce recipes, artifacts, materials, LODs, repair candidates, and structured visual review.
systems.proof: End-to-end prop, furniture, weapon, plant, creature, ship, building, and environment validation atlas.

systems.purpose: Articulation, deformation, contacts, locomotion, behaviour, interpolation, and temporal cadence.
systems.proof: Stability, purpose, contact, topology, recognisability, and temporal-LOD tests.

systems.purpose: Import promoted canonical output into a future selected runtime engine.
systems.proof: Clean-project import, build, profiling, identifier preservation, and replay tests.
